title: Propagate GitHub token into canonical conversation secrets
description:
Problem
After removing `/run`, the GitHub Worker no longer forwards the resolved GitHub token to the agent-server conversation path. That means tools running inside the canonical conversation path cannot use `GITHUB_TOKEN`, even though the Worker already resolved the correct installation or user token.

Why this matters
- GitHub-aware tools need the token inside the agent runtime, not just in the Worker.
- This was previously available through the bespoke `/run` envelope and got dropped during the refactor.
- Gemini correctly flagged this as a real regression.

Desired outcome
The canonical conversation path should receive the GitHub token as a secret, using the same `GITHUB_TOKEN` name that the agent-server already knows how to register.

title: Add local machine and GitHub invocation context to agent-server environment information
description:
Problem:
The canonical agent-server conversation path does not currently inject local machine facts or GitHub invocation metadata into the system prompt.

Desired outcome:
Use the existing system-prompt environment-information seam to tell the agent:
- local repo layout (repos live under ~/repos, smolpaws lives at ~/repos/smolpaws)
- the resolved workspace root for this conversation
- GitHub invocation context such as repo, event type, actor, and issue/PR number when present

Constraint:
Keep this simple. Do not add a new prompt system or a full skills-loading subsystem just for this.

title: Fix /setup skill copy: reference SmolPaws + OpenHands (not Claude)
description: Update `.agents/skills/setup/SKILL.md` so wording consistently references **SmolPaws** and **OpenHands** (agent-sdk/runner), removing any Claude-specific branding/model mentions unless explicitly required. Ensure setup steps match current repo architecture (WhatsApp host, GitHub ingress worker, shared Fastify agent-server) and don’t imply vendor lock-in.

title: Support notifications-path mentions outside app-installed repos
description:
Problem
A mention in a repo where the GitHub App is not installed should still be able to trigger SmolPaws through the notifications polling path, but the current implementation has multiple blockers.

Observed case
- `@smolpaws` mention in `enyst/.openhands` issue #4 did not trigger a reply even though the `smolpaws` GitHub account can see the notification.

Current blockers
- `GITHUB_USER_TOKEN` is not configured in the Worker secrets, so scheduled notification polling currently returns early.
- The notifications cron is now hourly, so even with a token the path is delayed by design.
- The current notification handler expects `subject.latest_comment_url` and ignores threads without it, which likely skips issue-body mentions in non-app-installed repos.

Desired outcome
The notifications path should reliably process mentions from allowed authors in allowed repos even when the GitHub App is not installed there, including issue-body mentions where that is a real notification shape.

title: Integrate ToM-SWE (tom-swe) into TypeScript agent-sdk (tom_consult parity)
description:
Background
- “Tom agent” is ToM-SWE: https://github.com/OpenHands/ToM-SWE (PyPI: tom-swe).
- Python OpenHands Software Agent SDK uses it via openhands-tools:
  - openhands/tools/tom_consult/definition.py + executor.py
  - dependency: tom-swe>=1.0.3 (openhands-tools/pyproject.toml)
  - example: agent-sdk/examples/01_standalone_sdk/30_tom_agent.py

What the Python SDK does today
- Adds TomConsultTool + SleeptimeComputeTool to the agent tool list.
- Passes config params (enable_rag, llm_model, api_key, api_base).
- TomConsultExecutor formats conversation history into LLM-provider message dicts (role + content blocks) and calls:
  - tom_swe.tom_agent.create_tom_agent(...)
  - agent.give_suggestions(user_id, query, formatted_messages)
  - agent.sleeptime_compute(sessions_data, user_id)
- Persistence surface for both OpenHands + Tom is under ~/.openhands (conversations + user modeling files).

Goal
- Bring equivalent Tom integration to the TypeScript SDK SmolPaws is based on: ~/repos/oh-tab/packages/agent-sdk.

Proposed TS implementation
1) Add new tools to TS agent-sdk:
   - tom_consult: consult ToM-SWE for guidance
   - sleeptime_compute: index conversation history for user modeling
2) Implementation approach: spawn a Python helper (python3) from the TS tool that imports tom_swe and runs create_tom_agent + give_suggestions/sleeptime_compute.
   - Inputs passed as JSON (stdin) to avoid shell quoting issues.
   - Outputs returned as JSON and mapped to a TS observation shape { suggestions, confidence?, reasoning? }.
3) Conversation history formatting:
   - Build formatted_messages from ToolContext.events.list() by extracting MessageEvents (skip system).
   - Preserve the OpenHands message shape: { role, content: [{ type: "text", text: "..." }] }.
4) Configuration:
   - Expose enable_rag, llm_model, api_key, api_base (and optionally user_id) as tool params/constructor options.
   - If python3 or tom-swe is missing, return an agent-visible error explaining how to install: pip install tom-swe.
5) Docs / parity:
   - Update oh-tab/packages/agent-sdk/docs/python-parity.md (it currently lists Tom Consult Tools as Python-only).
6) SmolPaws wiring:
   - Bump @smolpaws/agent-sdk once published.
   - Decide whether to include Tom tools by default or behind a feature flag.

Acceptance criteria
- TS agent-sdk can execute a tom_consult tool call end-to-end when python3 + tom-swe are installed.
- Missing dependency errors are actionable and do not crash the runtime.
- No hard dependency on tom-swe for installs/tests unless explicitly enabled.

title: Adopt OpenClaw-style SmolPaws context docs and load the always-on ones
description:
Problem
SmolPaws had a canonical root SOUL and some repo/user skills, but it did not yet carry the broader OpenClaw-style workspace file family that helps define a persistent agent identity and human/environment context. We also lacked a clear separation between docs that should always enter the prompt and docs that need extra runtime infrastructure such as heartbeats or startup hooks.

Desired outcome
Add a docs/smolpaws/ context-file set adapted from the OpenClaw templates, load the always-on files (AGENTS.md, IDENTITY.md, USER.md, TOOLS.md) into the live agent context from the canonical local SmolPaws repo, document the rest as reference-only for now, and cover the behavior in agent-server tests.

title: Adapt /setup to run WhatsApp onboarding end-to-end
description:
Problem
Current setup guidance and/or automation does not reliably take a fresh machine from zero to a working WhatsApp connection for SmolPaws. We need a single path that adapts setup to the WhatsApp host in this repo and actually runs the necessary steps.

Goal
Make the setup flow (skill + any supporting code/scripts) walk through and execute WhatsApp onboarding end-to-end for this repo’s architecture.

Scope (proposed)
- Verify prerequisites (Node version, deps installed).
- Ensure required config/secrets exist (WhatsApp auth/session storage paths, any keys).
- Start the WhatsApp host in a way that can display/emit the QR code and confirm successful pairing.
- Persist the session so subsequent runs reconnect without re-auth.
- Validate by sending/receiving a test message and confirming routing into the agent runtime.
- Provide clear failure modes and log pointers (where to look, what to retry).

Acceptance criteria
- Running the setup flow in a clean checkout can complete WhatsApp pairing and persist credentials.
- After setup, the recommended dev command connects without re-pairing.
- A test inbound WhatsApp message triggers the agent and produces a reply.
- Skill text matches actual behavior (no stale vendor-specific wording).

comments.author: enyst
comments.text:
# WhatsApp details (SmolPaws)

This is a living scratchpad of everything we currently know about WhatsApp ingress in **SmolPaws**, what’s working, what’s broken, and what the next steps should be.

> Note: This file intentionally contains **no secrets**. When it references config/env, it only names files/variables.

## 0) What we’re trying to achieve

- Use a **personal WhatsApp account** as an ingress channel for SmolPaws.
- Pair once (QR scan), then reconnect on restart using persisted auth.
- For now: run everything **locally**, without Apple Container / Docker.
- Longer-term: containerize later.
- Desired architecture: WhatsApp ingress should work **like GitHub ingress** — i.e. send a prompt to the **agent-server** using the `/api/conversations` HTTP API and then deliver the assistant reply back to WhatsApp.

## 1) Current code layout (relevant files)

- WhatsApp host:
  - `src/index.ts`
  - Connects via Baileys, stores metadata/messages, routes to agent runtime.
- WhatsApp pairing script:
  - `src/whatsapp-auth.ts`
  - Script entrypoint: `npm run auth`
- Storage config:
  - `src/config.ts`
  - Exports `WHATSAPP_DIR`.
- SQLite:
  - `src/db.ts`
  - Creates/uses `messages.db`.
- Agent execution:
  - `src/agent-runtime/*`
  - Currently implemented via `src/agent-runtime/shared-runner.ts` (Apple Container workspace + agent-server inside that workspace).

## 2) Where WhatsApp data is stored on disk

All WhatsApp persistence is host-only (outside the repo):

- **WhatsApp auth state (Baileys multi-file auth):**
  - `~/.smolpaws/whatsapp/auth/`
  - Contains credentials + Signal key material.
  - Treat as a secret (anyone with this can act as the linked device).

- **WhatsApp message & scheduler DB (SQLite):**
  - `~/.smolpaws/whatsapp/messages.db`
  - Tables include:
    - `chats` (jid/name/last_message_time)
    - `messages` (id/chat_jid/sender/sender_name/content/timestamp/is_from_me)
    - `scheduled_tasks`, `task_run_logs`

Storage behavior:
- Chat metadata is stored for all chats (group discovery).
- Full message content is only stored for **registered** chats/groups.
- Media bytes are not stored (only text + captions).

## 3) Current runtime situation (local machine observations)

### Container runtime
- Apple Container is **not installed** on this machine.
- Docker is installed but **not desired** for now.

### Agent-server
- There is already an agent-server process running locally:
  - Observed `tsx src/runner.ts` under `apps/agent-server/`.
  - Listening on `127.0.0.1:8788`.

This is good: it matches how GitHub ingress dispatches via `/api/conversations`.

### LLM profile configuration (important)
The agent-server requires an LLM profile id:
- It reads `LLM_PROFILE_ID` from env, or
- falls back to VS Code settings: `openhands.llm.profileId`.

On this machine:
- VS Code setting `openhands.llm.profileId` is set to `gpt-5`.
- The `gpt-5` profile points to a local base URL (`http://127.0.0.1:55716`) which was **not running** at the time we checked.

Implication:
- Even if WhatsApp pairing succeeds, the agent may not be able to run until we point the agent-server to a working LLM profile and ensure required keys are available.

## 4) WhatsApp pairing status + the failure mode we found

### What happened
- `npm run auth` initially failed immediately with “Connection failed. Please try again.”
- The `~/.smolpaws/whatsapp/auth/` directory existed but was empty (no creds persisted yet).

### Diagnosed failure
A verbose Baileys run showed:
- Connection error: `Connection Failure`
- Disconnect status code: **405**
- The failure occurred during “not logged in, attempting registration…”, before QR was emitted.

### What fixed it
When we explicitly set Baileys’ WhatsApp web version:

- `version = await fetchLatestWaWebVersion()`
- pass `version` into `makeWASocket({ version, ... })`

…it successfully produced a QR code in the terminal.

So the fix is to make sure our code always supplies a fresh WA web version (or updates frequently).

## 5) Why pairing is not enough (will it run?)

Pairing makes WhatsApp connectivity work, but the system still needs:

1) A running **agent runtime** (today: agent-server)
2) A working **LLM configuration** (profile + tokens)
3) WhatsApp → agent dispatch plumbing that does **not require Apple Container** (local-only)

Right now, WhatsApp ingress **routes into** `runAgentRuntime()` which is implemented via `shared-runner.ts` that always tries to create an Apple Container workspace:

```ts
Workspace({ kind: 'apple', ... })
```

That will fail without Apple Container, regardless of WhatsApp pairing.

So we need a local-mode execution path.

## 6) Target architecture (where we’re going)

### Guiding principle
Make WhatsApp behave like GitHub ingress:
- GitHub worker calls agent-server directly:
  - `POST /api/conversations`
  - `POST /outbound_messages/claim`
  - `GET /events/search?...`

### Proposed local-only approach
- Keep `apps/agent-server` running on the host.
- Update WhatsApp host (`src/index.ts` / agent runtime) to **dispatch to agent-server** at `http://127.0.0.1:8788` (configurable).
- Use the same auth scheme as GitHub ingress:
  - `Authorization: Bearer $SMOLPAWS_RUNNER_TOKEN` (optional if only bound to localhost).

This avoids Apple Container completely.

### Why agent-server is still relevant in “local mode”
Even in local mode, agent-server gives:
- a stable conversation API surface
- persistence, event search, tool execution plumbing
- consistent behavior across WhatsApp and GitHub ingress

## 7) Concrete work items to complete WhatsApp ingress (local mode)

### A) Fix pairing flow to be reliable
- Update `src/whatsapp-auth.ts` to:
  - call `fetchLatestWaWebVersion()`
  - pass `version` into `makeWASocket({ version, ... })`
- Consider updating `src/index.ts` similarly for initial connect.

### B) Add a local agent-runtime implementation
Goal: stop using Apple Container when we’re in local-only mode.

Options:
1. **Use agent-server HTTP** from WhatsApp host (recommended)
   - Implement an agent-server client in `src/agent-runtime/` similar to `apps/github/src/agentServerClient.ts`.
   - Wire `runAgentRuntime()` to choose between:
     - local agent-server dispatch (default for now)
     - shared-runner (Apple Container) later

2. **Use `LocalConversation` directly** (bypassing agent-server)
   - This is possible because `@smolpaws/agent-sdk` exports `LocalConversation`.
   - But it would diverge from GitHub ingress behavior and likely increase “two runtimes” complexity.

### C) Ensure LLM profile + provider key are configured
- Confirm where the “provider key” should live.
  - We suspect a host-side env file like `~/.smolpaws/.env` (mentioned) is intended.
  - We must load it without printing secrets.
- Ensure agent-server has a valid `LLM_PROFILE_ID` or VS Code-selected profile that actually works.

### D) End-to-end validation checklist
- Pair WhatsApp (QR scan) and verify auth files appear under `~/.smolpaws/whatsapp/auth/`.
- Start agent-server locally and confirm it is alive:
  - `http://127.0.0.1:8788/api/alive` (or whichever endpoint exists in runner).
- Start SmolPaws WhatsApp host:
  - `npm run dev`
- Register a test chat/group (`data/registered_groups.json`).
- Send `@Andy hello` (or chosen assistant trigger) and confirm:
  - message stored in SQLite
  - host dispatches prompt to agent-server
  - assistant reply is received and posted back to WhatsApp

## 8) Notes / gotchas

- Baileys can emit the QR multiple times; the auth script should not spam the terminal too hard.
- A successful pairing should create multiple files in `~/.smolpaws/whatsapp/auth/` (not just one).
- WhatsApp registration failures may look like “Connection Failure” with status codes (e.g. 405) — keep logs around for diagnosis.
- If we bind agent-server to non-loopback hosts, `SMOLPAWS_RUNNER_TOKEN` becomes mandatory.
- Do **not** mount `~/.smolpaws/whatsapp/` into any agent container.

## 9) Where we are right now

- WhatsApp auth directory exists: `~/.smolpaws/whatsapp/auth/`.
- We can generate a QR code successfully **when** we pass `version` from `fetchLatestWaWebVersion()`.
- Agent-server is running on `127.0.0.1:8788`.
- WhatsApp host currently depends on Apple Container via `shared-runner.ts`.
- LLM profile selection currently points to a likely-dead local endpoint (`gpt-5` profile baseUrl), so agent execution may still be blocked until the LLM is configured.

---

## Appendix: Useful commands (no secrets)

### Check agent-server port
```bash
lsof -nP -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN | rg ':8788'
```

### Check WhatsApp persistence
```bash
ls -la ~/.smolpaws/whatsapp
ls -la ~/.smolpaws/whatsapp/auth
```

### Run pairing
```bash
npm run auth
```

### Run WhatsApp host
```bash
npm run dev
```

title: Keep WhatsApp host alive via LaunchAgent and bootstrap local agent-server when needed
description:
Problem
The WhatsApp host only runs when started manually, even though it now depends on the same local agent-server path as GitHub.

Desired outcome
Run the WhatsApp host continuously as a LaunchAgent and ensure local message handling can start the local agent-server automatically if it is down.

Key notes
- Use launchd RunAtLoad + KeepAlive semantics, not StartInterval.
- Reuse the same ~/.smolpaws/.env loader as the existing local scripts.
- Bootstrap the local agent-server only for local runner URLs; do not try to spawn arbitrary remote URLs.
- End state is a Mac-local always-on WhatsApp host that still routes through the canonical local agent-server.

title: Document the agent-server runtime test harness and coverage
description:
Problem
We now have realistic agent-server runtime tests and CI coverage, but the repo docs do not yet explain how the harness works, how to run it locally, or what behavior it intentionally covers.

Desired outcome
Document the new agent-server runtime test harness, local commands, and the coverage boundaries so future work can extend the same testing style instead of drifting back to narrower helper-only tests.

title: Find and enter a hackathon
description: Scout hackathons, pick one, register, build something fun with the agent. See docs/hackathon-hunt.md for scouting results. Top candidates: Auth0 Authorized to Act (Apr 7) and Agents Assemble (May 11).

title: Set up X (Twitter) account for smolpaws
description: SmolPaws should have a presence on X. Set up an account, figure out the voice/posting approach, and eventually connect it as a channel or at least a place to share things from.

title: Finish neutral scope naming and remove remaining group/main aliases
description:
Problem
The runtime model has been moved to neutral execution scopes, but the WhatsApp host/runtime seam still carries legacy aliases like `groupFolder` and `isMain` in `src/agent-runtime/types.ts`, `src/index.ts`, and `src/task-scheduler.ts`. The mount-security path also still uses `isMain` terminology.

Why this matters
- The codebase conceptually moved from tenancy-by-group toward trusted scopes, but the runtime payloads still tell two stories.
- This makes the remaining security and task code harder to reason about than it needs to be.

Desired outcome
Use `scopeId` / `isControlScope` consistently at the host/runtime boundary and keep any truly unavoidable legacy naming isolated behind one compatibility seam, or remove it entirely if nothing external still needs it.

title: Fail closed on missing GitHub actor allowlist and require auth for non-localhost runner binds
description: The GitHub Worker currently treats a missing ALLOWED_ACTORS config as allow-all, and the local agent-server can be started bound to all interfaces with auth disabled. Hardening needed: (1) webhook and notifications ingestion must fail closed when ALLOWED_ACTORS is missing or empty, and (2) the runner must default to loopback binds and reject non-localhost binds unless SMOLPAWS_RUNNER_TOKEN is configured.

title: Refresh incoming secrets when reusing a live conversation
description:
Problem
`createConversationRecord(...)` returns early for an existing live conversation and only refreshes the SmolPaws config. New incoming secrets on `POST /api/conversations` are ignored in that path.

Why this matters
- GitHub installation tokens are short-lived and must be refreshed across comments.
- Even if the Worker starts sending `GITHUB_TOKEN`, reused conversations would still keep the stale or missing secret.
- This is a general canonical conversation bug, not just a GitHub-only detail.

Desired outcome
When `POST /api/conversations` reuses a live conversation, the server should also apply the incoming secrets to that conversation before the next user message runs.

title: Support LLM profiles and per-profile API keys in the standalone agent-server
description:
Problem
The standalone smolpaws agent-server currently reads direct env LLM settings only. It does not accept or resolve profile IDs against ~/.openhands/llm-profiles, and it does not know how to load per-profile API keys outside the VS Code extension host.

Why this matters
- Local single-model startup is fine for now, but multi-model use still works only in the extension path.
- We want the standalone runtime to stay simple while still supporting the same profile selection model when needed.
- A later VS Code secret bridge may help, but the runtime should first know how to resolve profile config at all.

Desired outcome
The standalone agent-server should be able to accept a profile id, load profile config from ~/.openhands/llm-profiles, and use a matching local secret source for the provider key, without depending on the VS Code extension process.

title: Add agent context note about conversation storage and naming
description:
Problem
The SmolPaws agent currently is not explicitly told, in its own runtime context, where conversations are persisted or how ingress-specific conversation ids are named. That makes it harder for the agent to find prior history intentionally when it needs to inspect its own logs.

Desired outcome
Add a short, always-on note in agent context describing where canonical conversation logs live and the naming pattern for common ingresses, including GitHub thread-scoped conversations and local/WhatsApp conversations.

Scope
- Mention the canonical conversation root under ~/.openhands/conversations
- Mention that GitHub conversations are thread-scoped (for example github-owner-repo-number)
- Mention that local/WhatsApp conversations use local-* ids
- Keep it brief and operational, not a long doc dump

title: Add an ingress contract test for the GitHub Worker -> agent-server conversation path
description:
Problem:
We have a live end-to-end GitHub path now, but there is no automated test proving the Cloudflare Worker queue consumer speaks the expected agent-server endpoints and request shapes.

Desired outcome:
Add a lightweight automated test that exercises the GitHub ingress queue-consumer path against the agent-server contract, especially:
- POST /api/conversations
- POST /api/conversations/:id/outbound_messages/claim
- fallback behavior when outbound messages are empty or prompt is blank

Why this matters:
This is the narrowest useful E2E seam before the full networked GitHub path, and it will catch contract drift between apps/github and apps/agent-server.

title: Notifications dedupe must allow repeated mentions on the same thread
description: The GitHub notifications poller currently dedupes only by GitHub notification thread id. After one @smolpaws mention on an issue or PR thread, later mentions on the same thread are skipped for the dedupe TTL even when latest_comment_url changes. Dedupe should key on the actual mention target so each new mention on the same thread enqueues once.

title: WhatsApp ingress delivers duplicate messages to agent
description:
Engel sends a single message on WhatsApp, but the agent receives it multiple times (observed as duplicate conversation triggers). This has happened repeatedly — the agent processes the same message 2-3+ times, leading to redundant replies.

Symptoms:
- Agent responds to the same message multiple times
- Particularly noticeable when WhatsApp delivery is slow or the agent takes time to respond
- The duplicate seems to arrive as a separate trigger, not a retry

Likely areas to investigate:
- WhatsApp message polling loop in src/index.ts (getNewMessages / timestamp tracking)
- Message deduplication by message ID vs timestamp
- Race condition between message processing and timestamp advancement
- Possible re-delivery from the WhatsApp connection (Baileys reconnection / message upsert)

title: Add heartbeat ingress so HEARTBEAT.md can wake smolpaws proactively
description:
Problem
We are adopting the OpenClaw-style context file family under docs/smolpaws, but HEARTBEAT.md is only meaningful if SmolPaws has a periodic ingress that invokes the agent with a heartbeat prompt and lets it act or stay quiet. Right now smolpaws has GitHub webhooks/notifications and WhatsApp ingress, but no dedicated heartbeat path that reads HEARTBEAT.md.

Desired outcome
Add a small proactive heartbeat ingress for SmolPaws so HEARTBEAT.md becomes operational rather than just documentary. Keep it aligned with the current agent-server and queue model rather than inventing a parallel runtime.

title: (idea) SmolPaws extension system inspired by Pi
description:
This bead recreates the old GitHub-side idea from `enyst/smolpaws#1`, but it is explicitly for the canonical `smolpaws/smolpaws` repo now.

Goal:
Design a lightweight extension system for SmolPaws, centered on the shared agent-server/runner surface rather than the old standalone GitHub repo.

Original motivation from the old issue:
- Pi-style simple extension entrypoint
- lifecycle hooks around interesting seams (run start, tool call, tool result, run end)
- shareable packages/manifests for tools, skills, prompts, and templates
- support for both local scripts and reusable packaged extensions

Current SmolPaws framing:
- canonical repo is `smolpaws/smolpaws`
- shared runtime surface now lives in the SmolPaws repo (WhatsApp + GitHub ingress converge on the same local agent-server-compatible runtime)
- any extension system should attach at the runner/agent-server boundary and work for both ingress channels

Questions to answer later:
- runner-only extensions vs ingress-specific hooks too
- per-repo vs global vs per-scope extension loading
- capability/safety model for tool interception and credential-sensitive hooks
- whether `send_message` and task tools should be part of the extension capability surface or remain core

This is a memory/parking-lot item for future design work, not an immediate implementation task.

title: Add chat ingress paths for Slack and Discord
description:
Problem
SmolPaws currently has working GitHub and WhatsApp ingress paths, but Slack and Discord are only mentioned as ideas in requirements and are not tracked as concrete ingress work.

Desired outcome
Define Slack and Discord as first-class future ingress paths that route into the same local agent-server conversation surface used by GitHub and WhatsApp.

Scope
- Keep the same SmolPaws conversation model and outbound messaging semantics
- Reuse the shared agent-server path instead of adding a separate runtime
- Treat channel-specific auth, webhook/event intake, and reply delivery as ingress adapters

title: Recover stale smolpaws conversations generically across ingresses
description:
Problem
Only WhatsApp currently resets reused conversations that are already stuck in waiting_for_confirmation or max_iterations_exceeded. GitHub and any future ingress still reuse stale conversations blindly because the canonical agent-server create/reuse seam does not recover them.

Desired outcome
When SmolPaws reuses a conversation for a new incoming message, the canonical agent-server path should detect stale waiting_for_confirmation and exhausted conversations and reset them generically, so all ingresses get the same recovery behavior.

Scope
- Implement at the shared agent-server conversation reuse boundary, not only in a specific ingress client.
- Cover both waiting_for_confirmation and max_iterations_exceeded recovery.
- Preserve normal conversation reuse for healthy idle/running flows.

title: Turn Daytona into a workspace backend instead of a special /run path
description:
Problem
Daytona still lives as a special case inside `apps/agent-server/src/agent-server/conversationRouter.ts` via `maybeRunInDaytona(...)`, instead of being modeled as a workspace backend parallel to local Apple-container execution.

Why this matters
- It keeps host lifecycle logic inside the request route instead of the workspace layer.
- It diverges from the Python reference model, where workspaces own runtime lifecycle and the agent-server stays generic.
- It makes the `/run` path carry deployment concerns that should be below the conversation boundary.

Desired outcome
Introduce a Daytona-backed workspace path so both local and Daytona execution target the same agent-server surface, with workspace selection contained in one place and `/run` no longer owning Daytona-specific branching.
notes: Intentionally deferred while we stabilize and test the local plain-runner path. Keep the target architecture aligned with the Python workspace model: workspace owns lifecycle, agent-server stays generic, Daytona becomes a sibling backend to AppleWorkspace rather than a special /run branch.

title: Extract shared runner protocol/types out of agent-server source imports
description:
Problem
`apps/github` imports `../../agent-server/src/shared/github.js` and `../../agent-server/src/shared/runner.js` directly from the agent-server source tree. That works inside the repo, but it means the GitHub app depends on the internal file layout of another app rather than a neutral shared boundary.

Why this matters
- It makes app boundaries fuzzier than they look.
- It raises the cost of refactoring the agent-server package layout.
- It makes future packaging/independent builds more brittle.

Desired outcome
Move the shared queue/runner protocol types and schemas to an explicitly shared module/package owned by `smolpaws`, then make both `apps/github` and `apps/agent-server` consume that boundary instead of cross-importing app internals.

title: Add Slack ingress path
description:
Parent: ingress expansion for SmolPaws.

Problem
Slack is listed in requirements but not implemented.

Desired outcome
Support Slack mentions/messages as an ingress that starts or reuses SmolPaws conversations through the shared local agent-server, with replies delivered back into the same Slack thread/channel context.

Scope
- Slack event/webhook intake
- actor/channel allowlisting and auth
- channel/thread-scoped conversation ids
- outbound send_message delivery back to Slack

title: Load AgentContext and skills in the agent-server conversation path
description:
Problem
The GitHub and agent-server paths now use the canonical /api/conversations lifecycle, but createConversationRecord(...) still does not attach an AgentContext. That means repo, user, and public skills are still absent from the first LLM request even on the canonical path.

Why this matters
- The route shape is now correct, but the runtime still lacks one of the key Python-style context seams.
- The first LLM turn still misses repo/user/public skill context.
- This is now the main gap behind tool/profile alignment for local repo work.

Desired outcome
The agent-server conversation path should construct and attach an AgentContext that loads the appropriate local skills for the resolved workspace, following the Python model as closely as the TS SDK allows.
notes:
Python reference direction:
- the agent-server exposes a first-class `/skills` endpoint (`skills_router.py`) that loads and merges public, user, org, sandbox, and project skills before the conversation starts
- `StartConversationRequest` then carries an `agent` object that can already include `agent_context`
- TS follow-up should aim at the same shape: a first-class skills-loading seam plus conversation creation that carries the resulting AgentContext, instead of inventing another custom request shortcut

title: Restore real agent capabilities on the standalone /run path
description:
Problem
The current local standalone `/run` path in `apps/agent-server/src/agent-server/conversationRuntime.ts` creates `LocalConversation` with `includeDefaultTools: false`, no `AgentContext`, and only the scope-bound `send_message` tool. That means GitHub-triggered runs can talk back to the thread, but they cannot actually inspect files, run commands, edit code, or load skills on the plain local path.

Why this matters
- The live GitHub mention path currently dispatches through `/run`, so this is the behavior users will see first.
- Even after we bypass Daytona, the agent still will not be able to do real repo work until this path gets a proper tool/context profile.
- It also makes the first LLM request much thinner than the rest of the SDK surface, which is easy to miss when testing only the transport.

Desired outcome
The standalone `/run` path should provision the same meaningful local capabilities we expect from SmolPaws for repo work: default tools (or an explicit workspace-aware profile), plus any required AgentContext/skills for the scope.

title: Add a local shell script for starting the agent-server with required env validation
description:
Problem:
We keep restarting the local agent-server ad hoc, and it is easy to forget required env like LLM_MODEL, provider keys, or runner token.

Desired outcome:
Add a small checked-in shell script to start the local agent-server with clear env validation and stable defaults for local development/testing.

Why this matters:
It should be obvious how to bring up the local runner before testing GitHub/Worker integration.

title: Add agent-server tests in the same style as OpenHands-Tab agent-sdk
description:
Problem:
The smolpaws agent-server currently has only narrow local tests around specific helpers, while the OpenHands-Tab agent-sdk has stronger prompt/runtime-style tests that validate behavior more directly.

Desired outcome:
Adopt the same testing habits where sensible: focused runtime tests that capture first-request prompt shape, tool exposure, skills loading, and conversation behavior without unnecessary scaffolding.

Constraint:
Keep the test style close to the OpenHands-Tab agent-sdk approach instead of inventing a second testing culture here.

title: Add Discord ingress path
description:
Parent: ingress expansion for SmolPaws.

Problem
Discord is listed in requirements but not implemented.

Desired outcome
Support Discord mentions/messages as an ingress that starts or reuses SmolPaws conversations through the shared local agent-server, with replies delivered back into the same Discord channel/thread context.

Scope
- Discord gateway/webhook intake
- actor/channel allowlisting and auth
- channel/thread-scoped conversation ids
- outbound send_message delivery back to Discord

title: GitHub local runs should resolve a repo workspace, not use one ambient runner root
description:
Problem
GitHub-triggered local runs still start from one ambient default working directory (`~/repos/smolpaws`) unless the request explicitly sets `workspace.working_dir`. That means mentions on repos like `enyst/.openhands` can execute against the wrong local checkout even though the GitHub event already tells us which repo triggered the run.

Why this matters
- GitHub ingress is supposed to target arbitrary allowed repos on the local machine.
- Project skills already try to follow the GitHub repo name, so the working directory and the skill root can drift apart.
- This is the main remaining functional gap before local GitHub execution is trustworthy across multiple repos.

Current evidence
- `apps/github` sends GitHub repo metadata, but no explicit `workspace.working_dir`.
- `apps/agent-server/src/agent-server/conversationRuntime.ts` still falls back to the default working dir when no request working dir is present.
- Live tests on `enyst/.openhands` started in `/Users/enyst/repos/smolpaws` instead of the target local clone.

Desired outcome
GitHub-triggered local runs should resolve the local repo workspace automatically:
- explicit `workspace.working_dir` wins when present
- otherwise prefer a repo override from `~/.smolpaws/repo-map.json`
- otherwise use a same-name local clone under `SMOLPAWS_WORKSPACE_ROOT`
- otherwise fall back to the configured default working dir

The same repo resolution should also be reused for project-skill loading so the agent starts in the same repo whose skills it sees.

title: Load local agent-server secrets from ~/.smolpaws/.env
description:
Problem
The local smolpaws runner currently relies on ad hoc shell exports for model keys and runtime GitHub credentials, which makes local bring-up fragile and inconsistent with the idea of a stable local runtime identity.

Desired outcome
The checked-in local runner launcher should automatically load ~/.smolpaws/.env when present, so local runtime secrets like LLM_MODEL, OPENAI_API_KEY, and GITHUB_TOKEN are available without per-shell manual exports.

title: Add /api/llm-profiles CRUD to the smolpaws agent-server
description:
Problem
The standalone smolpaws agent-server still has no REST surface for listing, reading, creating, updating, or deleting named LLM profiles. That is not the immediate blocker for local profile resolution, but it is a natural follow-up if we want remote clients to manage reusable named profiles without pushing full inline LLM payloads each time.

Desired outcome
Add a deferred `/api/llm-profiles` CRUD surface in the smolpaws agent-server as a thin wrapper over the underlying profile store, with redacted reads and a clean path toward profile-backed conversation start/switch/restore later.

Notes
- This is intentionally deferred behind the current standalone profile-resolution work.
- Keep it aligned with the Python design direction, but adapt it to the TypeScript SDK/profile model we already have.

title: Add regression test for WhatsApp reconnect loop dedupe
Load user and project skills in agent-server conversations
description:
Problem:
The canonical smolpaws agent-server conversation path attaches environment info but does not load user skills or project-local skills.

Desired outcome:
Enable AgentContext user-skill loading and load project skills from the resolved repo directory, preferring the GitHub target repo for GitHub-triggered runs and falling back to the smolpaws repo.

Constraint:
Keep it simple and close to the Python project-skill model (.agents/skills, .openhands/skills, root files like AGENTS.md).

title: Reset exhausted WhatsApp conversations and raise local iteration cap
description:
Problem
WhatsApp self-chat conversations can get stuck forever after max_iterations_exceeded because the local agent-server path keeps reusing the same conversation id.

Desired outcome
If a WhatsApp conversation is exhausted, start a fresh local conversation automatically and use a much higher iteration cap for WhatsApp turns.

title: When outbound send_message is used, GitHub delivery should still post the final assistant reply
description:
Problem
The GitHub worker currently chooses between outbound thread messages and the final assistant reply. If a run emits any claimed outbound messages (for example a progress update via send_message), processQueueMessage only posts those outbound messages and never posts the final assistant reply from the conversation events.

Current evidence
- Live run for enyst/llm-playground#12 posted the intermediate comment "Let me check my starting directory and the project skills available."
- The local conversation log then completed with a final assistant answer identifying /Users/enyst/repos/llm-playground and the Conversation Persistence skill.
- dispatchToAgentServer currently returns early with outbound_messages whenever outbound claim returns non-empty, so the worker never fetches/post the final reply in that case.

Desired outcome
Deliver outbound thread messages first, then also deliver the final assistant reply when it exists and is not just a duplicate of the last outbound message.

title: Deduplicate the same GitHub mention across webhook and notifications ingress
Notifications poller should not miss recent mentions just because GitHub marked them read
description:
Problem
The GitHub notifications poller currently fetches only unread notifications via `GET /notifications?per_page=50`. If a mention notification is already marked read by the time the cron job polls, the Worker never sees it even though the notification still exists and still represents a fresh trigger.

Current evidence
- `enyst/OpenHands-Tab#1008`, `#1009`, and `enyst/llm-playground#11` all show up in the `smolpaws` account notification UI and in `GET /notifications?all=true`, but not in the unread-only API response the Worker currently uses.
- The Worker therefore misses those mentions entirely unless they are still unread at poll time.

Desired outcome
Poll a bounded recent window of notifications with `all=true`, keep the existing dedupe logic, and process recent mention notifications even if GitHub already marked them read. Avoid replaying stale historical mentions.
